notes: Pickleheads' city index page (pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/pleasanton) links to 5 distinct venue detail pages — Muirwood Community Park, Clubsport of Pleasanton, Bay Club Pleasanton, Lifetime Activities, and Pleasanton Middle School Gym — but its own synthesized summary states only '4 pickleball locations with 25 courts' and, separately, '2 locations with 7 indoor pickleball courts'. Both aggregate figures are explained by Pickleheads silently excluding the 'Clubsport of Pleasanton' page from its totals: 25 = 6 (Muirwood) + 9 (Bay Club) + 4 (Lifetime Activities) + 6 (Middle School Gym), omitting Clubsport's 9; and 7 indoor = 1 (Bay Club indoor) + 6 (Middle School Gym indoor), omitting Clubsport's 1 indoor court. This lines up with an independent finding below: Clubsport of Pleasanton and Bay Club Pleasanton are the same real-world facility (The Bay Club acquired ClubSport's Pleasanton club in Nov 2018; both listings cite the identical address, 7090 Johnson Dr, and identical court figures). Pickleheads appears to carry a stale duplicate page under the old brand name that it excludes from its own aggregate math but still serves individually — same pattern as the Ocean View Park dual-city-slug duplicate found during Berkeley research. Separately, Pickleheads has no page under the name 'Pleasanton Tennis and Community Park' (the name used in courts-data.json/venues.json) — that park's pickleball courts are instead listed under 'Lifetime Activities', the third-party operator that runs them, matched here by identical address (5801 Valley Ave). All 5 existing baseline Pleasanton entries are therefore accounted for on Pickleheads; notOnPickleheads is empty.

venues.name: Muirwood Community Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court — dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.hours: Open for play until dark; no lights (first-come, first-served)
venues.weather: Inland Tri-Valley — hot, sunny, especially summer afternoons
venues.notes: All corroborated fields (courts=6, Outdoor, Free, no-lights/dawn-to-dusk) match the existing baseline exactly — no contradictions, no gaps filled in the shared courts-data.json schema. Amenities list is new detail not tracked by the existing schema at all (not a 'fill', since courts-data.json has no amenities field), so this is recorded as existing-no-change rather than existing-update. Baseline's skill value ('2.5–5.0, welcoming all levels') was not independently corroborated by any source checked here, so it's left 'Not specified' in this record rather than assumed.

venues.name: Clubsport of Pleasanton
venues.surface: Hard and wood courts — dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets (8 outdoor, 1 indoor)
venues.weather: Inland Tri-Valley — hot, sunny, especially summer afternoons
venues.notes: IMPORTANT — likely a stale duplicate of the 'Bay Club Pleasanton' record below, flag for human review before merging. Pleasanton Weekly confirms The Bay Club acquired ClubSport's Pleasanton club (along with Walnut Creek, Danville, Fremont, and Portland ClubSport locations) in November 2018; multiple sources describe 'ClubSport Pleasanton' as 'now known as Bay Club' at the identical 7090 Johnson Dr address. Pickleheads' Clubsport page and Bay Club page both report the same 9 courts (8 outdoor + 1 indoor) figure, and Pickleheads' own city-level aggregate totals (see top-level notes) mathematically exclude this Clubsport page, consistent with Pickleheads treating it internally as superseded. courts (9) matches existing baseline exactly, so no numeric conflict is recorded here — this record instead fills in the previously-hedged address (baseline had 'exact address unconfirmed — distinct facility from Bay Club'), plus price/surface/reservable/amenities, which is why matchStatus is existing-update rather than existing-conflict. Recommend a human decide whether to keep this as a separate courts-data.json entry or fold it into bay-club-pleasanton. bookingUrl left null — no ClubSport-branded booking system still operates; reservations for this facility now run through bayclubs.com (see the Bay Club Pleasanton record's bookingUrl).

venues.name: Bay Club Pleasanton
venues.surface: Tennis-court surface with portable nets (8 outdoor, 1 indoor per Pickleheads)
venues.netType: Portable nets on tennis-court surface; balls and paddles available
venues.weather: Inland Tri-Valley — hot, sunny, especially summer afternoons
venues.notes: Two independent Pickleheads-sourced searches consistently report 9 total courts (8 outdoor + 1 indoor), which is the value recorded in 'conflicts' against the existing baseline's 8. Note for the human reviewer: a search grounded in bayclubs.com's own marketing page separately described '8 dedicated pickleball courts (4 indoor, 4 outdoor)' — a THIRD figure that agrees with neither Pickleheads (9, 8/1 split) nor the existing baseline (8, split unspecified beyond 'Both'). Given the conflicting sources, confirmed is left false and courts is recorded from Pickleheads per this file's scope, but the true current count is genuinely unclear and worth a phone call to the club to resolve. Hours match the existing baseline exactly. See the 'Clubsport of Pleasanton' record above — same address, same Pickleheads-stated court figure, likely the same real-world facility under a pre-2018-acquisition page Pickleheads hasn't retired.

venues.name: Lifetime Activities
venues.surface: Outdoor concrete — dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.hours: Mon–Fri 8am–10pm, Sat & Sun 8am–8pm (general facility hours; reservations open daily at 12:30pm)
venues.weather: Inland Tri-Valley — hot, sunny, especially summer afternoons

conflicts.existing: Medium — lines painted on existing tennis courts, bring your own net
conflicts.pickleheads: Outdoor concrete — dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets
Reservable — verified Pleasanton residents may book 8 days ahead, unverified residents/non-residents 7 days ahead, reservations open daily at 12:30pm

venues.notes: Pickleheads does not have a page under the name 'Pleasanton Tennis and Community Park'; it lists this location under 'Lifetime Activities', the third-party operator contracted to run the park's tennis/pickleball courts and reservation system. Matched to the existing baseline entry by identical address (5801 Valley Ave, Pleasanton, CA 94566) and identical lat/lon, not by name — id kept as the existing 'pleasanton-tennis-and-community-park' rather than minted fresh from the Pickleheads name, consistent with how matched venues were handled in the Berkeley research pass. courts (4) fills a previously-null baseline field. surface and reservable both contradict existing non-null baseline values (existing describes free walk-up play on painted tennis-court lines with a bring-your-own net; Lifetime Activities' own reservation-policy page describes dedicated permanent-net courts bookable up to 8 days ahead) — left as unresolved conflicts rather than picked a side. It's plausible both are simultaneously true (dedicated courts available both for advance reservation AND casual walk-up when unreserved), but no source stated that explicitly, so confirmed is left false. Facility hours shown are the operator's general hours (lifetimeactivities.com's own contact page gives a very slightly different variant, Sat 8am–9pm/Sun 8am–8pm, vs the Pickleheads-sourced Sat/Sun 8am–8pm used here), not confirmed as pickleball-specific.

venues.skill: Recreational & competitive courts split (3 rec + 3 competitive)
venues.weather: Inland Tri-Valley — hot, sunny, especially summer afternoons

conflicts.pickleheads: $3.75 resident / $4.50 non-resident (single visit), plus 6-visit and 15-visit multi-passes

venues.notes: courts (6), Indoor, and the 3-recreational/3-competitive skill split all match the existing baseline exactly. hours ('Not specified' in baseline) is filled in as Saturday 9am–12pm only. price directly contradicts the existing baseline's $4.50/$5.25 figures with a different $3.75/$4.50 figure plus multi-visit pass tiers baseline didn't have at all — recorded as an unresolved conflict rather than assuming one side is a stale/updated price. Passes must be purchased in advance online at pleasantonfun.com or in person at 200 Old Bernal Ave; no cash handled onsite, which may explain the price discrepancy if baseline's figure predates a fee schedule change.
